response.justification: Strong full-stack engineer with 16+ years of relevant experience and proven TypeScript/Node.js expertise, exceeding the 6+ year requirement. Current role explicitly includes AI agent infrastructure work, directly addressing a required skill. However, primary stack mismatches are notable: frontend background is Ember.js, not React; Nest.js not mentioned despite being core backend framework; specific testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Playwright) not cited. These are learnable quickly given the candidate's demonstrated architectural skills and software craftsmanship, but represent real gaps from day-one productivity.
strengths: 16+ years of full-stack development with consistent senior/staff-level positions; extensive TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple roles
Current AI agent infrastructure focus and MCP (Model Context Protocol) experience directly satisfies required technical skill; demonstrated ability to own cross-cutting infrastructure
Proven software craftsmanship mindset (TDD, mentoring, refactoring dojos, open source) and rapid technology mastery across diverse stacks (Java, DLang, C++, etc.)
concerns: React expertise not demonstrated; primary frontend experience is Ember.js. While frameworks are learnable, React is a primary stack requirement, not secondary
Nest.js not mentioned despite being primary backend framework; resume shows generic Node.js backend work without evidence of this specific opinionated framework
Specific testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Playwright) not explicitly mentioned; while TDD background is strong, unfamiliarity with job-specific test tools could slow initial velocity
